title: Bailiff Auctions – How Does the Sale of a Debtor's Assets Work?
description: A bailiff auction is a public sale of the debtor's seized assets conducted by a court bailiff. Learn how to participate, the rules, and what can be purchased.
content:
What Is a Bailiff Auction?

A bailiff auction (licytacja komornicza) is a formal public sale of a debtor's seized assets conducted by a court bailiff (komornik sądowy). The purpose is to raise funds to satisfy the creditor's claims. Bailiff auctions are governed by strict procedures under the Code of Civil Procedure and are open to anyone who meets the formal requirements.

Legal Basis

Bailiff auctions are regulated by the Code of Civil Procedure:

  • Arts. 867–879 CCP – auctions of movable assets,
  • Arts. 972–984 CCP – auctions of real property.

What Can Be Auctioned?

The subject of a bailiff auction may include:

  • Movable assets – vehicles, machinery, equipment, furniture, valuables, goods,
  • Real property – apartments, houses, plots, commercial premises, agricultural and forest land,
  • Receivables and property rights – company shares, stocks, intellectual property rights.

Auction of Movable Assets

Auctions of movables take place at the bailiff's office or at the storage location. The procedure includes: description and valuation by the bailiff, announcement of the auction (at least one week in advance), a deposit (rękojmia) of 10% of the estimated value from each bidder, and the auction itself. The opening bid is 75% of the estimated value in the first term and 50% in the second term.

Auction of Real Property

Real property auctions are more complex. The bailiff commissions an expert valuation, announces the auction at least two weeks in advance, accepts deposits of 10% of the assessed value, and conducts the auction. The opening bid is 75% of the assessed value in the first term and two-thirds in the second term. The court subsequently issues an order transferring title to the successful bidder.

Who Can Participate?

Any individual or legal entity may participate in a bailiff auction provided they pay the required deposit before the auction, are not the debtor or the debtor's spouse, and are not the bailiff, their employee, or the creditor's representative in the case.

Auction Notices

Upcoming auctions are published on the website of the National Council of Bailiffs (www.licytacje.komornik.pl) and on the notice boards of the bailiff's office and the district court.

Why Buy at a Bailiff Auction?

Bailiff auctions offer the opportunity to acquire real estate or valuable assets often below market value. Title is transferred by court order, ensuring full legal security for the transaction.
